title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
AFT Notice of Change (NOC)
An AFT Notice of Change (NOC) transaction is a non-financial transaction created by the financial institution that receives an AFT transaction. The NOC transaction informs the AFT Originator of changes to the payment information (i.e., the Transit Number or account number).
Automated Funds Transfer (AFT)
An Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) is a Debit or Credit Transaction. Debit Transactions are known as Pre-authorized Debits (PADs). Credit Transactions are known as Direct Deposits.
Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Re-presentment
An AFT Re-presentment is an AFT debit transaction that has been returned for the return reason code 901 â NSF, or code 908 â Funds Not Cleared, and is re-submitted on a one-time only basis.
Business Day
A Business Day is a weekday including Monday to Friday except for statutory holidays that fall on or are observed on a weekday.
Business PAD
A Business PAD is a Pre-authorized Debit drawn on the account of a Payor for the payment of goods or services related to a business or commercial activity of the Payor, including, but not limited to, payments between franchisees and franchisers, distributors and suppliers, and dealers and manufacturers.
Cash Management PAD
A Cash Management PAD is a Pre-authorized Debit drawn on the account of a Payor for the purpose of transferring, consolidating, or repositioning funds between the Payorâs account at one financial institution to their account at another financial institution, for the same business or closely affiliated businesses (e.g., transfers between a parent company and its subsidiary).
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Confirmation
A Confirmation is the mandatory Written Notice from the Payee to the Payor prior to the first PAD wherein the details agreed to by the Payor in setting up an Agreement with the Payee are communicated in accordance with CPA Rule H1.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
Credit Transaction
A Credit Transaction is a credit payment item exchanged in an AFT File as defined in CPA Standard 005. Also known as a Direct Deposit.
Debit Transaction
A Debit Transaction is a Pre-authorized Debit within the meaning of Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits, exchanged in an AFT File as defined in CPA Standard 005.
Direct Clearer
A Direct Clearer is a member of the CPA that meets the requirements of the association and settles for clearing items through a settlement account at the Bank of Canada.
Direct Deposit
A Direct Deposit is an AFT Credit Transaction such as a payroll deposit.
Due Date
The Due Date is the date on which the Payor is to be debited or the date on which the Payee is to receive payment.
Error Correction
An Error Correction corrects a transaction posted to the recipientâs account in error. Error Corrections apply to transactions and AFT files.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
File Creation Number
A File Creation Number is a consecutive number automatically assigned to an AFT file during preparation for transmission. Central 1 uses the File Creation Number for verification purposes because files must be received in sequential order.
Funds Transfer PAD
A Funds Transfer PAD is when funds are drawn on the account of a Payor at one institution and transferred to an account of the same individual at another financial institution (Payor and Payee are the same). A Funds Transfer PAD can include, but isnât limited to, registered savings plans, mutual funds, segregated funds, annuities, deposit accounts, cash accounts, and investment accounts.
Offsetting Transaction
An Offsetting Transaction is the Debit or Credit Transaction posted to the Originatorâs account that equals the total value of the individual Debit or Credit Transactions within an outgoing AFT file. It is also, a transaction that offsets an AFT item returned through the Online Return System (ORS), or the Batch Return Service, if the software doesnât create the Offsetting Entry.
Online Return System (ORS)
The Online Return System (ORS) is a web-based system that facilitates the return of AFT Transactions and the creation of AFT Notice of Change transactions.
Originating Direct Clearer
The Originating Direct Clearer delivers AFT files to another Direct Clearer. Central 1 is the Originating Direct Clearer for financial institutions in BC and Ontario.
Originator
An Originator is the financial institution or business member that creates an AFT file.
Originator ID
The Originator ID is a 10-digit number that identifies the Originator of AFT Transactions or the Originator of returned AFT Transactions.
Payee
The Payee is the person or entity to whom funds are to be paid.
Payment Originator
A Payment Originator is a business, association, government, or other entity that initiates AFT Transactions with the authorization from its Payee or Payor.
PaymentStream
AFT
PaymentStream
AFT allows users to create AFT Transactions and upload AFT files.
Payor
The Payor is the person or entity whose account is debited.
Personal PAD
A Personal PAD is a PAD drawn on the account of a Payor for payments such as, but not limited to, charitable donations, non-Member investment contributions, mortgage installments, utility bills, insurance premiums, membership fees, property taxes, credit card billings, lines of credit, loans, and payment for other consumer goods or services but not a Business PAD, a Cash Management PAD, or a Funds Transfer PAD.
Pre-authorized Debit (PAD)
A Pre-authorized Debit (PAD) is an AFT Transaction issued by a Payee or Member Payee that is drawn on an account of a Payor (e.g., mortgage payment, strata payment, etc.). See Debit Transaction.
Pre-authorized Payment (PAP)
See AFT Transaction.
Pre-notification
Pre-notification is the required Written Notice from the Payee or Member Payee to the Payor, indicating the amount and date of a Pre-authorized Debit (PAD). Pre-notification is required prior to the date of the actual PAD, and it is also required to make a change to the amount or date of a PAD.
Processing Direct Clearer
The Processing Direct Clearer is the Direct Clearer that receives AFT Transactions from an Originating Direct Clearer.
Recall
A Recall is a request to return an AFT Transaction that hasnât been posted to the receiverâs account. A Recall applies to individual Credit Transactions and to AFT files. Recalled AFT files can contain both credits and debits. Individual debits can only be recalled if Central 1 hasnât sent the transaction to the data centre of the Receiving Financial Institution.
Receiving Financial Institution
The Receiving Financial Institution is the financial institution that receives AFT Transactions from another financial institution.
Reject
A Reject is a transaction in the AFT file that canât be processed by Central 1, the other data centre, or the Receiving Financial Institution.
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
Transaction Recall
See Recall.
Written Notice
A Written Notice is any form of representing or reproducing words in visible form, including an electronic document, provided the electronic document is under the control of the intended recipient, the information contained in the electronic document is substantially in the same form as a paper copy, and the information contained in the document is accessible if requested.
END
